title: Monticalia schultzei
intro: Monticalia schultzei, also known as the "Sapotilla" or "Palo de Sapo", is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of Monticalia schultzei are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic effects. Monticalia schultzei is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Monticalia schultzei is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Monticalia schultzei among the many species of plants that produce composite flowers, characterized by a cluster of small florets surrounded by larger petals. Within the Asteraceae family, Monticalia schultzei is further classified within the subfamily Mutisioideae, which includes a range of plants native to the Americas. The genus Monticalia itself is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in South America, and Monticalia schultzei is one of the many species within this genus. Further classification of Monticalia schultzei may include its placement within a specific tribe or section within the Mutisioideae subfamily.
